description: Single-page React labyrinth app. Generates a maze inside a 500x800 area. Supports user input to draw a path from start to exit. Runnable locally via npm scripts.

userStories.title: Create empty React app scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an empty React app created in this repo so I can run and iterate locally.
acceptanceCriteria: Repository contains a React project scaffold created via standard tooling (CRA or Vite)
App runs locally with a single command (prefer: npm run start; acceptable: npm run dev if Vite, but add npm run start alias)
README includes exact commands for install and run (npm install; npm run start)
userStories.notes: Prefer Vite for simplicity; if Vite is used, ensure package.json has a 'start' script that maps to 'vite'.

userStories.title: Single-page UI layout with fixed maze area
userStories.description: As a user, I want a single page that contains a dedicated labyrinth area sized 500x800 so I can play consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: App is a single page (no routing required)
A visible container for the labyrinth is exactly 500px wide and 800px tall
Page shows a title and controls above the labyrinth area
Layout is responsive around the fixed labyrinth area (centered or aligned cleanly)
userStories.notes: Use CSS to enforce exact dimensions for the maze container.

userStories.title: Generate button and maze data model
userStories.description: As a user, I want a button to generate a new labyrinth so each game feels different.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Generate labyrinth' button exists and is clearly visible
Clicking the button creates a new maze (different layout most of the time)
Maze generation produces a data model (grid/cells/walls) stored in React state
Start and Exit positions are defined and rendered
userStories.notes: Use a standard maze algorithm (e.g., recursive backtracker). Keep the grid size reasonable for rendering performance.

userStories.title: Render labyrinth inside 500x800 area
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the labyrinth walls clearly rendered inside the fixed area.
acceptanceCriteria: Maze is rendered fully within the 500x800 container (no overflow)
Walls are clearly visible; paths are navigable
Start cell and Exit cell are visually distinct (icons or colors)
Rendering approach is deterministic from the maze state (Canvas or SVG/HTML grid)
userStories.notes: Canvas is recommended for drawing and input tracking; SVG also acceptable.

userStories.title: Input: draw a line from start to exit
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click/touch at the start and draw a continuous line through the labyrinth until I reach the exit.
acceptanceCriteria: Supports mouse input: pointer down, move, up to draw a line
Supports touch input (Pointer Events preferred) for drawing on mobile
Drawing starts only when user begins in the Start area/cell (or very close to it)
A drawn line is displayed in real-time as the user moves
A 'Clear path' action exists to reset drawing without regenerating the maze
userStories.notes: Use Pointer Events (pointerdown/move/up/cancel) to unify mouse + touch.

userStories.title: Collision rules: prevent drawing through walls
userStories.description: As a user, I want the game to prevent cheating by drawing through walls so the labyrinth is meaningful.
acceptanceCriteria: If the drawn path crosses a wall, the app detects it and blocks progress (either stop drawing, or mark as invalid and require restart)
Collision detection is reliable at typical pointer speeds
User receives clear feedback when they hit a wall (message or visual indicator)
User can restart drawing after a collision (auto-clear or via Clear button)
userStories.notes: If using Canvas, you can check sampled points against wall boundaries; if using grid model, check movement between cells for wall presence.

userStories.title: Win condition and feedback
userStories.description: As a user, I want to know when I successfully reach the exit.
acceptanceCriteria: When the drawn path reaches the Exit area/cell, the app shows a success state (message/modal/banner)
Success state offers actions: Generate new labyrinth, or draw again
No false positives: must actually enter the Exit area via valid path (no wall crossing)
userStories.notes: Keep the UX minimal but clear.

userStories.title: Polish: README + basic quality checks
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clear run instructions and basic checks so the project is easy to use and maintain.
acceptanceCriteria: README includes: prerequisites (Node/npm), install, run, and short usage instructions
Project includes consistent formatting/linting (minimal setup is fine)
No critical console errors in dev mode
App builds successfully (if build script exists) or at least runs reliably via npm run start
userStories.notes: Do not over-engineer; keep dependencies minimal.
